alternate_names: National Center of Incident Readiness and Strategy for Cybersecurity
notes: Japan's central cybersecurity policy coordination body and government CERT. Serves as secretariat of the Cybersecurity Strategic Headquarters (CSHQ), a Cabinet body established under the Basic Act on Cybersecurity (secid:regulation/cyber.go.jp/basic-act-cybersecurity). Monitors/analyzes threats to government information systems, publishes the national Cybersecurity Strategy and the Common Standards for Cybersecurity Measures of Governmental Entities, and coordinates with JPCERT/CC (which handles private-sector incident response) to form Japan's national CERT structure. Originally established 2005 as the National Information Security Center; took its NISC form in 2015 alongside the Basic Act on Cybersecurity; reorganized into the National Cybersecurity Office (NCO), headed by a National Cyber Director, in July 2025 following the Cybersecurity Capability Enhancement Act (May 2025). The legacy nisc.go.jp domain remains live as an archive.

match_nodes.description: National Cybersecurity Office — Japan's central cybersecurity policy body and government CERT (successor to NISC, July 2025)

data.notes: Headed by the National Cyber Director. Secretariat of the Cybersecurity Strategic Headquarters.

match_nodes.description: National Center of Incident Readiness and Strategy for Cybersecurity — predecessor name of the NCO (2015-2025)

data.official_name: National Center of Incident Readiness and Strategy for Cybersecurity
data.notes: Reorganized into the National Cybersecurity Office (NCO) in July 2025. Kept as a separate, lower-weight match_node since 'NISC' remains the name widely used in pre-2025 sources and citations.
